# MCP Conformance Tests - Base Messaging (JSON-RPC 2.0)
# This test validates the basic JSON-RPC message format requirements for MCP protocol

# Get the server command from the environment
server_command = $MCP_SERVER_COMMAND

# If stdio is not supported, skip this test file
[!stdio] skip "Server does not support stdio transport"

# Start the test server with mcp-serve
exec mcp-serve -- $server_command

# TEST 1: Request Format Requirements
# - Must include jsonrpc: "2.0"
# - Must include a string or integer ID (not null)
# - Must include a method field
# - Params are optional
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"ConformanceTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec mcp-send
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":1'
stdout '"result":'

# Send initialized notification
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec mcp-send

# TEST 2: Response Format Requirements
# - Must include jsonrpc: "2.0"
# - Must include the same ID as the request
# - Must include either result or error, not both
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"test response"}}
exec mcp-send
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":2'
stdout '"result":'
! stdout '"error":'

# TEST 3: Error Response Format
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"unknown_method"}
exec mcp-send
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":3'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":'
stdout '"message":'
! stdout '"result":'

# TEST 4: Notification Format (no ID)
# - Must include jsonrpc: "2.0"
# - Must include a method field
# - Must NOT include an ID field
# - Params are optional
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/test","params":{"value":"test notification"}}
exec mcp-send
# No response should be received for notifications

# TEST 5: Invalid request - Missing jsonrpc version
setstdin {"id":5,"method":"echo"}
exec mcp-send
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'

# TEST 6: Invalid request - Missing method
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":6}
exec mcp-send
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'

# TEST 7: Invalid request - Null ID (not allowed in MCP)
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":null,"method":"echo"}
exec mcp-send
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'

# TEST 8: Batch request support
[batch] setstdin [{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":801,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"batch1"}},{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":802,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"batch2"}}]
[batch] exec mcp-send
[batch] stdout '"id":801'
[batch] stdout '"result":'
[batch] stdout '"id":802'
[batch] stdout '"result":'

# TEST 9: Batch with mixed requests and notifications
[batch] setstdin [{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":901,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"batch_req"}},{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/test","params":{"value":"batch_notif"}}]
[batch] exec mcp-send
[batch] stdout '"id":901'
[batch] stdout '"result":'
# Only the request should get a response, not the notification

# Clean up: Stop the server
exec mcp-serve --stop
